TSLA: Who is Raj Jegannathan: Inside the journey of the Indian-origin Tesla VP who spent 13 years at Elon Musk’s company. Raj Jegannathan, an Indian-origin Tesla Vice President, has ended his nearly 13-year tenure at the company. He held various critical roles in technical, infrastructure, and sales leadership, playing a significant part in the company's internal operations and AI initiatives. His departure comes amidst Tesla navigating sales pressures and leadership changes.

Elon Musk’s Go-To Banker Is Back in Action for the SpaceX IPO. Michael Grimes, a prominent banker at Morgan Stanley, is reportedly returning to the firm after a stint in the Commerce Department to work on the potential SpaceX initial public offering. Grimes has a long history of working with Elon Musk, having previously helped take Tesla public. His return positions Morgan Stanley to play a leading role in what could be one of the largest IPOs ever.

Elon Musk Admits It Would Take 'Staggeringly Enormous Amount Of Work For Tesla Valuation To Hit $100 Trillion: 'I'm Just Saying…'. Tesla CEO Elon Musk has acknowledged that achieving a $100 trillion valuation for the company would demand a "staggeringly enormous amount of work and good luck," though he believes it's not impossible. Tesla's current market cap is $1.5 trillion, and his vision for growth beyond electric cars includes robotaxis, humanoid robots, and energy storage. These ventures, along with his recently approved trillion-dollar pay package and the shift to a subscription-only model for Full Self-Driving, are seen as key to reaching these ambitious valuation goals.

GM hires new head of strategy from Lucid Motors. General Motors has appointed Claudia Gast, formerly of Lucid Motors, as its deputy CFO and VP of strategy, corporate development, and technology partnerships. She will begin on March 1 and will focus on scouting partnerships with technology companies, reporting to CEO Mary Barra and CFO Paul Jacobson. Gast replaces former Tesla executive Zach Kirkman, who is leaving GM for other opportunities.

Easton mother sues Tesla after son dies trapped in burning car. An Easton mother has filed a wrongful death lawsuit against Tesla after her 20-year-old son, Samuel Tremblett, died trapped in a burning 2021 Tesla Model Y last October. The lawsuit alleges that defective electronic doors prevented Tremblett from escaping the vehicle after a crash, leading to his death from thermal injuries and smoke inhalation. His attorney claims Tesla has ignored previous reports of similar incidents, prioritizing profits over customer safety, with 15 deaths reported since 2016 due to occupants being trapped by electric doors.

'I'm going to die.' Easton mom sues Tesla, son died in burning car. An Easton mother has filed a wrongful death lawsuit against Tesla, alleging that a defect in the vehicle's electronic doors led to her 20-year-old son's death in a fiery crash. Samuel Tremblett died from thermal injuries and smoke inhalation in October 2025 after his Tesla Model Y collided with a tree and burst into flames, leaving him trapped inside. The lawsuit claims Tesla continued to manufacture vehicles with the dangerous door design despite internal concerns and numerous entrapment incidents.
TGT: Target CEO Fiddelke promotes two executives; 500 jobs cut - Bizwomen. Target's new CEO, Michael Fiddelke, has promoted Cara Sylvester to Chief Merchandising Officer and Lisa Roath to Chief Operating Officer as part of a leadership reshuffle. This reorganization comes as the retailer reportedly plans to cut an additional 500 jobs, following 1,800 eliminations last fall. Fiddelke aims to differentiate Target as a style and design leader and improve efficiency, with two senior executives also departing the company.

Target job cuts signal renewed focus on physical stores. Target has announced plans to cut approximately 500 corporate, regional, and supply chain roles to reallocate resources towards frontline staffing and store operations. This strategic shift aims to strengthen the performance of physical stores by investing in more scheduled hours for shop-floor staff and enhanced training. The move comes amid pressure on margins and increasing customer expectations, signaling a pivot towards a more store-centric operating model to improve customer service and sales conversion.

Report: Target increasing store staffing; cutting jobs in DCs, regional offices. Target is reportedly shifting its staffing strategy to enhance the in-store customer experience. The company plans to increase store labor investment while eliminating approximately 500 jobs across its distribution centers and regional offices, following a memo from its chief stores officer and chief supply chain officer. This change aligns with newly appointed CEO Michael Fiddelke's priority of elevating the "guest experience."

Target to test ads on ChatGPT this month. Target is planning to test placing advertisements on the ChatGPT platform this month. This initiative marks a new venture for the retail giant into AI-driven advertising spaces.

Target slashes 500 jobs as retailer seeks to invest in its stores. Target is cutting approximately 500 jobs across its regional offices and distribution sites in the US. This move, announced by executives in an internal email, aims to allow the company to invest more in its stores and enhance the customer experience. The job cuts are part of a broader strategy, including a reorganization of geographic store districts, to boost in-store staffing and reverse stagnant sales experienced over the past four years.

Target To Cut 500 Regional Office And Distribution Jobs, Pour More Money Into Stores: Report. Target Corporation is reportedly cutting about 500 jobs in its distribution centers and regional offices while increasing investment in store staffing and training. This move aims to address customer complaints about store conditions, out-of-stock items, and long checkout lines, following several years of flat sales and increased competition. The changes are part of a broader strategy under new CEO Michael Fiddelke to simplify operations and improve the in-store experience.

Ulta Beauty CEO joins Pinterest board as it builds AI shopping platform. Pinterest has appointed Kecia Steelman, CEO of Ulta Beauty, to its Board of Directors, effective February 16, 2026. This move is strategic as Pinterest aims to evolve into a leading visual discovery and AI-powered shopping platform, especially targeting Gen Z. Steelman brings extensive experience in growing global businesses, omnichannel experiences, and brand partnerships to support Pinterest's growth initiatives.

Target To Reportedly Beef Up Store Staffing, Cut 500 Jobs Elsewhere To Address Customer Complaints. Target (TGT) is reportedly overhauling its store operations to improve customer experience by increasing store staffing and cutting approximately 500 jobs across its supply chain and district levels. The restructuring, driven by newly-appointed CEO Michael Fiddelke, aims to address customer complaints about store conditions and long checkout lines. This move follows previous corporate layoffs in 2025 and seeks to channel more resources into enhancing the in-store shopping experience for customers.

Target increasing investment in store labor to clean up stores, win back customers (TGT:NYSE). Target (TGT) is reallocating its labor resources to improve the in-store customer experience. The company plans to increase staffing in its stores to address issues such as store appearance, long lines, and lack of customer support, while simultaneously cutting 500 jobs in its distribution centers and regional offices. This move aims to clean up stores and win back customers who have complained about declining store conditions.
SBUX: ProShare Advisors LLC Has $120.27 Million Position in Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. ProShare Advisors LLC recently reduced its stake in Starbucks Corporation by 18.6%, now holding over 1.4 million shares valued at $120.27 million. Despite this reduction, other institutional investors like Norges Bank and Invesco Ltd. significantly increased their positions in the coffee company. Analysts currently rate Starbucks as a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $104.67, following the company's recent earnings report which missed consensus estimates.
DIS: The Walt Disney Company $DIS Stock Holdings Increased by Profund Advisors LLC. Profund Advisors LLC increased its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 21.9% during the third quarter, bringing their total holdings to $3.187 million. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Disney. Analysts currently rate Disney as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus target price of $135.80, following recent positive earnings and strategic announcements.

Fox Corp. (Class B): How a Legacy Media Powerhouse Is Rebuilding Its Flagship Equity Story. The article analyzes Fox Corp. (Class B) as a "pure play" investment in Fox's focused media strategy post-Disney asset sale, emphasizing its concentration on live news, live sports, and ad-supported entertainment via Fox News, Fox Sports, and Tubi. It highlights how this share class offers investors exposure to Fox's cash flows without the Murdochs' voting control, contrasting it with more diversified and streaming-heavy competitors. The piece argues that Fox's disciplined capital allocation, strategic clarity, and high-margin content mix provide a competitive edge in a volatile media landscape.

ESPN and Disney Launch ‘We’re Going,’ the First Marketing Campaign for ESPN’s Inaugural Super Bowl. ESPN and Disney have launched "We're Going," a marketing campaign for ESPN's inaugural Super Bowl in February 2027. The campaign reimagines the classic "I'm Going to Disney World" tradition, featuring over 60 Disney characters journeying to Super Bowl LXI. This initiative marks the beginning of a year-long promotion, emphasizing the collaborative storytelling power of Disney and ESPN.
NKE: RPT-With Puma stake, China's Anta seeks to enter the arena with Nike and Adidas. China's top sportswear company, Anta Sports, is expanding its global presence through an aggressive acquisition strategy, including a recent $1.8 billion stake in Puma. Having surpassed Nike and Adidas in the Chinese market, Anta aims to become the world's largest sportswear conglomerate. The Puma deal is crucial for Anta to gain a foothold in the global mass market, particularly in the U.S. and Europe, where its own brand has limited recognition outside Asia.
